description_long: This is a stone relief sculpture depicting a reclining female figure, possibly a mythological or allegorical character, holding a large conch shell to her lips. The figure is partially draped, with flowing hair and fabric rendered in detailed, high-relief carving. The sculpture is set within an irregular, roughly triangular slab, with the back side left flat and unfinished. The front surface is intricately sculpted, while the sides and back are plain and unadorned. The overall appearance suggests a classical or neoclassical style, with attention to anatomical detail and expressive posture. The object appears weathered, indicating it may have been displayed outdoors or is an older artifact.
description: A detailed stone relief sculpture of a reclining female figure holding a conch shell, carved in high relief on a roughly triangular slab. The front is intricately detailed, while the back and sides are plain and unfinished.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full reclining female figure with flowing hair and drapery, holding a conch shell to her lips, with detailed carving visible.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the sculpture's thin profile and the depth of the relief, with the figure's arm and body projecting slightly from the slab.
description_view_2: The back view displays a flat, unadorned surface, showing the unfinished stone and the overall triangular shape of the slab.
description_view_3: The right side view again highlights the thinness of the relief and the protruding elements of the figure, with minimal detail visible from this angle.
attribute_subject_matter: reclining female figure with conch shell
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the background is neutral and does not indicate a particular environment.
